title: Factories Act 1948 §41B
text: Occupier of a factory involving hazardous processes must disclose information on dangers and health hazards, maintain an on-site emergency plan, and lay down measures for handling, usage and disposal of hazardous substances.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41C
text: Occupier must maintain accurate health records of workers exposed to hazardous processes and appoint persons with prescribed qualifications to supervise handling of hazardous substances.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41D
text: Central Government may appoint an inquiry committee into occurrences involving extraordinary situations in factories engaged in hazardous processes.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41F
text: Maximum permissible threshold limits of exposure to chemical and toxic substances in manufacturing processes shall not exceed the values in the Second Schedule.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41H
text: Workers have the right to warn the occupier about imminent danger of serious bodily injury or death; the occupier must take immediate remedial action and inform the Inspector.

title: OISD-STD-105 Work Permit System
text: Hot work, confined-space entry and other hazardous jobs in oil/gas installations require a formal work permit specifying gas-test results, isolation, PPE and authorised signatories before work starts.
